name: Nifty 50
summary: Day 71 Saturday (today markets closed). **NIFTY 24,176.15 (-0.6% / -151.50 1D MAY 8 CLOSE); SENSEX 77,328.19 (-0.7% / -516 1D); BANK NIFTY 55,310.55 (-1.3% LEADING THE DECLINE) — INDIA TAKING IT WHILE GLOBAL EQUITIES MAKE RECORDS (S&P +0.84% RECORD, NASDAQ +1.7%, KOSPI +0.1% AT +36.5% MoM, MSCI EM +2.0%).** India is in the WORST position of major economies for this crisis: import-dependent, FII-dependent, entering earnings season, currency under pressure, oil at $101. **Causal chain (~65% crisis):** Brent at $101 → India imports ~85% of crude → CAD widens → INR ₹94.47 (vs pre-crisis ₹87.92, -7.5% to date); 1M -2.4% confirms persistent pressure → India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0%, +6-8 bps cycle as oil inflation intensifies. **FII outflows: ₹340.89 cr May 8 net vs DII +₹441 cr; ₹6,961.75 cr month outflows; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD 2026 — ALREADY EXCEEDED FULL-YEAR 2025 (₹1.66 LAKH CR).** FPI ownership in Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~90% of FII selling but the gap is widening. Bank Nifty -1.3% leading the decline = financials pricing both NIM compression and FII channel withdrawal. Index heavyweight drag: HUL, TCS, ITC, Reliance, Bajaj Finance, Sun Pharma down. **Q4 FY26 earnings season Indian-bank NIM compression** + domestic political/budget overhang pre-monsoon. **Falsification:** Brent below $90 sustained for 10 sessions = INR back toward 92-93, FII flows positive = Nifty back to 25,000+. **If FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, the DII channel cracks.** AI flows continue to bypass India IT services (Apple-Samsung-Intel halo to Korea/Taiwan/US). 200-DMA at ~24,500 still capping; structural breakout requires (a) MOU signature within Trump 1-week window (closes May 14) or Sadjadpour 14-day (May 21), (b) FII rotation reversal, (c) ₹93-handle USD/INR.
